topic.prompt_en: Describe weather or a season that affects your mood or routine.
topic.learning_goal: Learn to describe weather or a season through sensory detail, routine change, and personal effect.

angles.title: Choose one season or weather pattern
content: Pick something specific such as early autumn or rainy afternoons.
Avoid trying to cover the whole year.

angles.title: Describe the feeling of it
content: Use temperature, light, pace, and clothing to make it real.
Keep the detail practical rather than poetic.

angles.title: Explain how it changes your routine
content: Show what you do differently in that season or weather.
Small changes make the answer believable.

angles.title: End with mood or preference
content: Explain why you enjoy it or why it suits you.
A personal reason is stronger than saying it is nice.

sequence.focus: What kind of weather or season
sequence.target: Name it clearly and say when it usually happens.
Describe what it feels like and how it changes your day.
Explain why it affects your mood in a positive or negative way.

high_score_tips: A familiar season often works better than a dramatic one because the details are easier to support.
Describe how the weather changes what you wear, do, or feel.
Keep the language sensory but controlled.
If you talk about preference, explain why it fits your personality or lifestyle.

content.problem: Only saying the season is beautiful
content.why_it_hurts: The answer stays too general and does not sound personal.
content.fix: Add practical details such as the air feeling cooler, fewer people outside, or walking more often.

content.why_it_hurts: The weather sounds disconnected from your life.
content.fix: Show one thing you do differently because of that season.

content.why_it_hurts: The answer sounds like a report instead of spoken preference.
content.fix: Finish with why that weather suits your mood or pace.

content.why_it_hurts: The response sounds memorised if it stays at the level of general ideas and never lands on one lived detail.
content.fix: Add one concrete moment, reaction, or small consequence that actually happened to you.

content.problem: Reflection that only repeats the opening
content.why_it_hurts: The ending feels flat if it only restates the topic instead of adding insight or change.
content.fix: Use the final lines to show what changed, what you realised, or how you would respond now.

language.wrong_example: The weather are cool and comfortable.
language.better_example: The weather is cool and comfortable.

language.problem: Using too much like with weather
language.wrong_example: I like autumn because it is like comfortable.
language.better_example: I like autumn because it feels comfortable and calm.
language.reason: Feels comfortable is the natural pattern here.

language.problem: Overusing very or really with basic adjectives
language.wrong_example: It was very good and really important for me.
language.better_example: It turned out to be more meaningful than I expected.
language.reason: A precise evaluation sounds more natural than stacking intensifiers on basic adjectives.

language.problem: Repeating I think in every sentence
language.wrong_example: I think it is useful, and I think it matters because I think it affects daily life.
language.better_example: What makes it useful to me is the way it affects daily life.
language.reason: Removing repeated I think makes the answer sound more confident and more fluent.

core.example: The temperature is usually mild in early autumn.
There is often a light breeze in the evening.
The air feels crisp but not cold.
I do not enjoy weather that is too humid.
It is the kind of season when you can layer comfortably.
The cooler air feels refreshing after summer.

extended.note: Useful when the season feels reliable and comfortable.
Good for describing how a season changes your mood.
Useful for calm mornings in pleasant weather.
Natural for comparing with hot or humid weather.
Good for gentle seasonal transitions like early autumn.

extended.item: in a better frame of mind
extended.note: Strong but teachable phrase for mood effects.

core.example: I like it when the weather cools down a little after summer.
I tend to go for longer walks in that season.
The cooler nights help me sleep more comfortably.

core.phrase: put me in a better mood
core.example: That kind of weather usually puts me in a better mood.
The air feels easier to breathe when it is less humid.
Autumn suits my pace better than summer.

extended.phrase: there is a subtle shift in the air
extended.meaning_zh: 空气里有一种微妙的变化
extended.example: In early autumn, there is a subtle shift in the air.

extended.note: Good for comparing hot and mild weather.
extended.example: I feel less physically drained when the weather is mild.

extended.phrase: have a calming effect on me
extended.note: Strong phrase for weather and seasons that improve mood.
extended.example: That kind of weather has a calming effect on me.

extended.meaning_zh: 让日常生活感觉更轻松
extended.note: Good closing phrase for weather preference.
extended.example: It makes everyday life feel lighter somehow.

framework.goal: Describe weather or a season through feeling, routine change, and preference.

moves: Name the season or weather pattern.
Say when it usually appears and your general feeling toward it.
Describe what it feels like in practical terms.
Explain how it changes your routine or comfort level.
Say why it suits or does not suit you.
Keep the final point personal and grounded.

delivery_markers: What I enjoy most about it is ...
That is why this season suits me so well.

samples.answer:
The season I want to talk about is early autumn. I like it because the weather becomes cooler after summer, but it is not cold yet, so it feels comfortable.

What I enjoy most is the air. It is usually mild and a little crisp, especially in the morning and evening. Because of that, I spend more time outside. I often go for longer walks, and I also sleep more comfortably at night.

Another reason I like early autumn is that it puts me in a better mood. Summer in my city can be very hot and humid, so by the time autumn arrives, I feel more relaxed and less tired. It is not a dramatic change, but it makes everyday life easier for me.
highlights: Links weather to walking and sleep

band_guide.focus: Clear weather detail and one routine effect
opening: The season I want to talk about is ...
closing: It makes everyday life easier for me.

upgrades.improved: The weather is cooler, mild, and a little crisp.
upgrades.why: Specific weather words make the picture clearer.
upgrades.note: Use two or three sensory words, not ten.

upgrades.improved: It puts me in a better mood and helps me feel less tired.
upgrades.why: This gives the effect more substance.

upgrades.original: It was very important to me.
upgrades.improved: What made it important was the effect it had on the way I handled similar situations later.
upgrades.why: This explains importance through a concrete effect instead of a vague adjective.
upgrades.note: Useful when you want a simple but natural reflective sentence.

upgrades.original: I learned a lot from it.
upgrades.improved: I came away from it with a clearer idea of what I would do next time.
upgrades.why: This sounds more spoken and more specific than a flat learned a lot.
upgrades.note: Good for endings that focus on takeaway and future behaviour.

samples.answer:
The season I would like to describe is early autumn, mainly because it changes the pace of my daily life in a very gentle way. In my city, summer tends to be hot, heavy, and humid, so the first few weeks of autumn always feel refreshing.

There is usually a light breeze, cooler evenings, and a more settled kind of weather overall. I find that I naturally go outside more, whether that means walking instead of taking transport for short distances or simply spending more time outdoors after work. I also feel less drained physically, which improves my concentration.

What I like most is that early autumn has a calming effect on me. It is not dramatic or exciting, but it creates a softer rhythm in my day. That is probably why it is the season I look forward to most every year.
highlights: Season described through pace and routine

opening: The season I would like to describe is ...
closing: It creates a softer rhythm in my day.

upgrades.improved: It creates a softer rhythm in my day.
upgrades.why: This is more reflective and personal.

upgrades.improved: I feel less drained physically, which improves my concentration.
upgrades.why: The effect becomes more precise and believable.
upgrades.note: Great for work or study context.

upgrades.improved: What helped most was that it changed the way I judged the situation in practice.
upgrades.why: This sounds more mature and more natural than the vague phrase helped me a lot.
upgrades.note: Useful when you want to show growth without sounding dramatic.

upgrades.improved: It gave me a more balanced way to look at similar situations afterwards.
upgrades.why: Balanced way to look at is a more idiomatic reflective phrase in spoken English.
upgrades.note: Works well in opinion, process, and experience answers.

samples.answer:
If I had to choose one kind of weather that genuinely affects my mood, it would be the beginning of autumn. What appeals to me is not just the temperature itself, but the subtle shift in how the day feels. The air is cooler, the light is softer, and everyday movement seems slightly less rushed.

After a long summer, that change feels almost corrective. I sleep better, walk more, and generally approach the day in a calmer frame of mind. It is the sort of weather that makes me want to step outside rather than escape into air-conditioning, which already says a lot about the difference it makes.

I think that is why I value it so much. It does not transform my life in any dramatic sense, but it makes ordinary routines feel lighter and more manageable. For me, that quiet effect is far more valuable than dramatic weather I only enjoy from a distance.
highlights: Band 8 answer uses restraint and subtle contrast
Quiet effect is a strong, teachable closing

band_guide.focus: Subtle sensory detail and mature reflection
opening: If I had to choose one kind of weather ...
What appeals to me is not just ...
body: After a long summer, that change feels ...
It is the sort of weather that ...
closing: It makes ordinary routines feel lighter ...

upgrades.improved: It puts me in a calmer frame of mind.
upgrades.why: This sounds more natural and precise.
upgrades.note: A useful Band 8 phrase for emotional effect.

upgrades.improved: That quiet effect is far more valuable to me.
upgrades.why: The closing is reflective without sounding dramatic.

upgrades.improved: What stayed with me was not the event itself, but the shift it created in my judgment afterwards.
upgrades.why: This upgrade sounds more nuanced and more reflective than a broad claim like it changed me.
upgrades.note: Strong for higher-band closings that need subtlety rather than drama.

upgrades.improved: Its real value lay in the way it quietly reshaped my response patterns over time.
upgrades.why: Quietly reshaped and over time make the reflection sound more precise and more natural.
upgrades.note: Useful when you want a sophisticated closing without sounding academic.

practice.task: Describe a season or kind of weather that affects your mood or routine.
checklist: Name the season or weather clearly.
Describe what it feels like in practical terms.
Explain one routine change and one personal effect.
self_prompts: What does the air or light feel like?
What do you do differently then?
How does it affect your energy or mood?
